course.title: Deliver — Mental Performance for Endurance Athletes
course.description: Unlock your brain and get out of your own way. 6 modules, 30 lessons of evidence-based sport psychology tools for endurance athletes.
course.author: Endure Labs

lessons.title: The Chimp and The Coach
lessons.description: Your brain has two systems — one reacts, one decides. Performance lives in the gap between them.

lessons.title: Your Brain Is the Limiter
lessons.description: Your brain throttles output to protect you. The point where you think you've given everything is a signal, not a wall.

lessons.title: The Arousal Dial
lessons.description: Your best performance lives in a specific zone on the arousal dial — and that zone is different for every athlete.

lessons.title: The 6-2-7
lessons.description: Diaphragmatic breathing is the fastest bridge from the reactive brain to the deliberate brain.

lessons.title: Attention — The Spotlight You Control
lessons.description: Your attention is a spotlight — broad or narrow, internal or external. You control where it points.

lessons.title: Module 1 Quiz
lessons.description: Test your understanding of the two-brain system, arousal, breathing, and attention.

lessons.title: The Identity Tree
lessons.description: You will never outperform your self-concept. The words after 'I am' set the ceiling.

lessons.title: Where Confidence Actually Comes From
lessons.description: Confidence isn't a personality trait. It's a judgment based on available evidence.

lessons.title: The Perfectionism Trap
lessons.description: High standards + self-compassion = growth. High standards + self-punishment = burnout.

lessons.title: Performance Statements
lessons.description: You can't control your thoughts. But you can answer them.

lessons.title: The Athletic Schema Statement
lessons.description: Your self-schema is the thoughts and feelings you have about yourself as an athlete. Choose it, or it chooses you.

lessons.title: Module 2 Quiz
lessons.description: Test your understanding of identity, confidence, perfectionism, and self-talk.

lessons.title: Visualization Is Not Daydreaming
lessons.description: Mental imagery is a full-sensory cognitive rehearsal with decades of research behind it.

lessons.title: The Personal Highlight Reel
lessons.description: The act of visualizing contains the ergogenic value — watching video of yourself is not enough.

lessons.title: Disaster Planning
lessons.description: If-Then plans convert coping decisions into pre-programmed responses.

lessons.title: Self-Talk — The Soundtrack
lessons.description: System 1 self-talk is reactive. System 2 self-talk is planned. You can choose which runs.

lessons.title: Detachment in Action
lessons.description: Mindfulness: paying attention, on purpose, in the present moment, nonjudgmentally.

lessons.title: Module 3 Quiz
lessons.description: Test your understanding of visualization, highlight reels, disaster planning, self-talk, and mindfulness.

modules.title: The Zone and The Red Line

lessons.title: What Flow Actually Is
lessons.description: Flow is not about trying harder. It's what happens when the trying stops and the doing takes over.

lessons.title: Clutch vs. Flow
lessons.description: Flow is effortless. Clutch is deliberate. The deciding mile is usually clutch.

lessons.title: Motivation — The Engine Under Everything
lessons.description: Intrinsic motivation is the engine. When it runs dry, no external fuel replaces it.

lessons.title: The Data Trap
lessons.description: When wearables override body signals, you lose the ability to read yourself.

lessons.title: Training Monotony and The Unmotivated Day
lessons.description: Mental fatigue is neurological, not a character flaw.

lessons.title: Module 4 Quiz
lessons.description: Test your understanding of flow, clutch, motivation, data, and mental fatigue.

lessons.title: The Night Before
lessons.description: The night before is the first phase of competition. Preparation replaces anxiety.

lessons.title: Race Morning
lessons.description: A Pre-Performance Routine eliminates decision-making when your brain is least equipped.

lessons.title: The First Hour
lessons.description: The first hour is where discipline separates execution from impulse.

lessons.title: Deliver
lessons.description: This is clutch territory. Maximum deliberate effort. The moment every tool was built for.

lessons.title: Fueling Your Head
lessons.description: Nutritional anxiety is a distinct psychological construct — not just 'eat right.'

lessons.title: After The Line
lessons.description: Structured debrief, not rumination. Process the race, then put it away.

lessons.title: Module 5 Quiz
lessons.description: Test your understanding of the race-day protocol from night-before through post-race debrief.

lessons.title: The Emotional Rollercoaster Is Normal
lessons.description: The emotional rollercoaster is not weakness. It's a sign that this mattered to you.

lessons.title: Stress Is Physical
lessons.description: Mental stress amplifies physical pain. The vicious cycle is real.

lessons.title: Coping — What Actually Works
lessons.description: Problem-focused when you can control it. Emotion-focused when you can't. Avoidance always backfires.

lessons.title: Injury — The Uninvited Teacher
lessons.description: Injury threatens identity. If the athlete branch is the whole tree, losing it is existential.

lessons.title: Mental Toughness Is the Sum
lessons.description: Mental toughness is the integration of every tool in this course.

lessons.title: The Habit, Not The Goal
lessons.description: Daily micro-practice beats weekly deep sessions. The tools work — but only if you use them.

lessons.title: Module 6 Quiz
lessons.description: Test your understanding of adversity, coping, injury, mental toughness, and habit formation.
